verdict.answer_first: A states the fix in sentence 2: "split your dependency install from your source copy"; B has the recommendation in the heading/sentence 2: "fix layer-cache ordering" after a general opener.
verdict.decisiveness: A keeps one first move: "Do that first" and parks later levers as secondary; B adds "Then, two quick wins" plus timing command, creating more competing actions.
verdict.fabrication: B invents an unverifiable number: "~90%"; both assert "No Dockerfile in this repo" without the question documenting a repo.
verdict.actionability: A gives the savable prescription cleanly: manifest copy -> install -> source copy -> build, plus stack translations; B is useful but adds extra branches before the first fix is fully isolated.
